title: approvals — published events
description: Every event is a readonly value object dispatched afterCommit unless the trigger requires synchronous downstream work (execution, security). ApprovalGranted → ApprovalInstanceExecutor listener runs SYNCHRONOUSLY in the same request to preserve the caller's transaction context; every OTHER downstream listener is queued so a slow email dispatch doesn't block state transitions.

events.name: ApprovableActionRegistered
events.trigger: Boot-time discovery pass upserted a new `approvable_actions` row from an #[AsApprovableAction] decorator on an action class. Fires once per new registration per boot. Not fired on unchanged rows.
consumers: activity::WriteToActivityLog (platform-level activity feed — new capability visible)

events.name: ApprovalTemplateCreated
events.trigger: Tenant admin authored a new template. Version = 1 always.

events.name: ApprovalTemplateUpdated
events.trigger: Tenant admin edited a template's IMMUTABLE-post-instance fields (name, priority, description) — no version bump. Distinct from ApprovalTemplateVersionBumped which fires on when_expression / group changes.

events.name: ApprovalTemplateVersionBumped
events.trigger: Tenant admin edited a template's semantic-behaviour fields (when_expression, groups[].selector_expression, groups[].quorum, groups[].sort_order, on_timeout, allows_recall) — the template's version column increments AND a new row is written (old row preserved for audit).

payload.prior_approval_template_id: ULID (the OLD row's id, is_active=false now)
payload.name: string

events.name: ApprovalTemplateActivated
events.trigger: Tenant admin flipped is_active=true on an inactive template row. The one-is-active invariant enforced by ApprovalTemplateObserver deactivates any peer row with the same (tenant, application, action_key, name).

payload.name: string
payload.deactivated_peer_id: ULID | null (the row previously carrying is_active=true for the same key)

events.name: ApprovalTemplateDeactivated
events.trigger: Tenant admin flipped is_active=false OR peer activation deactivated this row via the observer. Distinct from soft-delete (still-active-but-hidden vs archived-permanently).

events.name: ApprovalTemplateGroupsUpdated
events.trigger: A group was added/edited/removed on a template. Batched — one event per admin edit session (multiple group mutations coalesce to one event with `changed_groups[]`). ALWAYS accompanied by ApprovalTemplateVersionBumped in the same transaction.

events.name: ApprovalRequested
events.trigger: The `approve` middleware intercepted a decorated action + resolved a matching template + created an `approval_instances` row with status='pending'. This is the fanout signal for NotifyApproversJob.
consumers: notifications::NotifyApproversJob (dispatches ApprovalRequestedNotification to each resolved approver)

events.name: ApprovalRequirementResolved
events.trigger: Per-requirement fanout of ApprovalRequested. Fires ONCE per requirement at instance-create time after the ApproverSelectorEngine materialised `resolved_approvers`. Consumers use this to build per-approver notification recipients without re-querying.

payload.starts_pending: boolean (false = requirement starts as blocked pending a lower sort_order group)

events.name: ApprovalDecisionRecorded
events.trigger: An approver POSTed approve OR reject and the ApprovalDecisionRecorder wrote a new `approval_decisions` row. Fires BEFORE quorum evaluation runs.

events.name: ApprovalRequirementSatisfied
events.trigger: Quorum reached on one requirement (after ApprovalDecisionRecorded's quorum-recompute). Downstream requirements with next sort_order may unblock.

payload.unblocked_requirement_ids: ULID[] (requirements transitioned pending→blocked→pending because this satisfied their sort_order predecessor)

events.name: ApprovalRequirementFailed
events.trigger: A reject decision was recorded on a requirement whose quorum demands unanimous approval (`quorum_type='all'`) OR the reject-with-quorum-any short-circuits the group. Consequence: the requirement transitions to failed AND the instance transitions to rejected.

events.name: ApprovalGranted
events.trigger: Every requirement has status='satisfied'. Instance transitions pending→approved. THIS IS THE ONLY EVENT DISPATCHED SYNCHRONOUSLY — the ApprovalInstanceExecutor listener runs in the same request context to preserve the caller's transaction. Downstream listeners (notifications, activity) queue as normal.
consumers: approvals::ApprovalInstanceExecutor (dispatches the underlying action with scoped('approvals.executing') flag)

payload.final_decider_id: ULID (the approver whose decision tipped the last requirement to satisfied)
payload.decision_count: integer (total approves + rejects that happened; usually equals requirement_count when all groups quorum=all)

events.name: ApprovalRejected
events.trigger: At least one requirement failed. Instance transitions pending→rejected. Distinct from ApprovalExpired (past expires_at) and ApprovalCancelled (requester withdraw).

payload.final_decider_id: ULID (the approver whose reject tipped the flow)

events.name: ApprovalExecuted
events.trigger: ApprovalInstanceExecutor successfully dispatched the underlying action + captured its return value. Instance transitions approved→executed AND `executed_at` is set. THIS IS THE HANDOFF SIGNAL to downstream cross-module bridges (access/grants materialization, billing charge processing, identity impersonation extension).
consumers: audit::WriteAuditRecord (P0 — this is the moment tenant state actually changed)

payload.execution_result_summary: Record<string, mixed> (the action's return value's PUBLIC-tier projection — never the full payload)

events.name: ApprovalExecutionFailed
events.trigger: ApprovalInstanceExecutor dispatched the underlying action but the action threw. The instance is in a WEDGED state: status='approved' but executed_at IS NULL. Human intervention required — retry via `approvals:remediate-failed-executions`.

payload.error_trace_fingerprint: string (SHA-256 of stack trace; correlate to Sentry)

events.name: ApprovalCancelled
events.trigger: Requester POSTed withdraw. Instance transitions pending→cancelled AND every pending requirement transitions to failed.
consumers: notifications::NotifyPendingApproversJob → ApprovalCancelledNotification (to the pending approvers, so they don't waste time deciding a withdrawn request)

events.name: ApprovalExpired
events.trigger: The ExpireOverdueApprovalsJob sweeper found an instance past expires_at with status='pending'. Final status depends on template.on_timeout: expire → status='expired'; approve → treats as unanimous approve + fires ApprovalGranted; escalate → new approvers added + expires_at extended.

events.name: ApprovalEscalated
events.trigger: template.on_timeout='escalate' AND the sweeper hit the timeout. New escalation approvers were resolved via template.escalation_selector + appended to the pending requirement's resolved_approvers. expires_at extended by template.expires_after_hours / 2.
consumers: notifications::NotifyApproversJob → ApprovalRequestedNotification to the new escalation targets

events.name: ApprovalReminderDispatched
events.trigger: DispatchApprovalRemindersJob wrote a new `approval_reminders` row AND dispatched an ApprovalReminderNotification to a specific approver. Fires ONCE per reminder — de-duplicated by the (requirement, approver, reminder_number) composite unique.

events.name: ApproverSelectorEvaluationFailed
events.trigger: ExpressionLanguageAdapter caught a parse error OR runtime error OR timeout evaluating a selector_expression at instance-create time. THE INSTANCE FAILS TO CREATE — the action itself refuses with 500 approval.selector_evaluation_failed. Operators + template author notified so they fix the template.
consumers: notifications::NotifyTemplateAuthorOfFailure (opt-in — the author of the template gets an email)

payload.error_message: string (redacted — no secrets, no context values)

events.name: DelegationSubstitutedApprover
events.trigger: During ApproverSelectorEngine::resolve(), an approver in the resolved set was substituted for their delegate because an active `access/delegation::RoleDelegation` covers the role/time-window. Fires PER substitution — a single instance-create with 3 delegated approvers fires 3 events.

payload.role_delegation_id: ULID (the access/delegation::RoleDelegation row that authorised this substitution)

events.name: ApprovalSecurityViolation
events.trigger: One of several P0 security signals: (1) someone POSTed approve/reject with a user_id NOT in the requirement's resolved_approvers (attempted forgery via bypass); (2) an ApprovalDecisionObserver caught a direct-DB write that would violate the resolved_approvers invariant; (3) an action's approvalContext() included regulated_secret / PCI-DSS PAN data (via downstream scanner). Fires ONCE per detected violation — de-dup at the (violation_type, source) level.

payload.details_fingerprint: string (SHA-256 hash for correlation without leaking specifics)
